title: agathelpis dubia
parts: Agathelpis dubia has many medicinal parts, such as the roots, leaves, and flowers, which are used in traditional medicine. The roots of the Agathelpis dubia plant are rich in bioactive compounds, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The leaves of the plant contain essential oils, including sesquiterpenes and monoterpene, which have been found to exhibit antimicrobial and antifungal activities. Additionally, the flowers of the Agathelpis dubia plant contain flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
side_effects: Agathelpis dubia can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea and vomiting, due to the high toxicity of its leaves and stems. Ingesting large amounts of the plant can also lead to cardiac problems, such as irregular heartbeat and palpitations, as well as respiratory issues like shortness of breath and wheezing. Additionally, improper use of Agathelpis dubia can cause skin irritation, including rashes and blisters, and may even trigger allergic reactions in some individuals. Furthermore, excessive consumption of the plant can lead to neurological symptoms, such as headaches and dizziness, due to its toxic compounds.
